calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar May-18-2026 Continued To Jul-20-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Petition To Probate Holographic Will
Off Calendar May-18-2026 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Ryan Hengl, for and with James Carter, Jr., Personal Representative.

The court acknowledges that no appearance by Petitioner Lisa Cleveland or her Attorney. The court takes the Petition to Probate Holographic Will off calendar. The court acknowledges the Response to the court's Order Show Cause (OSC) and the OSC is discharged. The court and counsel discuss the status of the Estate. At the conclusion of discussion, the matter is continued to Jul-20-2026 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 206.

Judge: Hon. Michael McNaughton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Petrucci. Not Reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-18-2026 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Petition To Probate Holographic Will
Off Calendar Mar-16-2026 Continued To May-18-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Elizabeth Erhardt for Lisa Cleveland (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on 1) Status on Close of Estate; and 2) Status on Petition to Probate Holographic Will.

Ms. Erhardt reported that she had not yet filed the anticipated petition to remove the current administrator and had not received any communication from him regarding the administration of the estate. Ms. Erhardt also raised concerns regarding attorney Ronald Berman's involvement in the matter, noting that he had previously appeared on behalf of different parties in the case and had recently filed a request for special notice indicating representation of California Inheritance Group, and questioned whether notice should be provided to him.

The Court noted that no petition is currently on file and directed counsel to file the appropriate petition and provide notice to all interested parties, including those who have requested special notice. The Court continued the matter to May 18, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 and ordered that an Order to Show Cause issue to administrator James Carter for failure to appear.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Mar-16-2026 Continued To May-18-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Dec-29-2025 Continued To Mar-16-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Elizabeth Erhardt for Lisa Cleveland (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Status on Close of Estate.

Ms. Erhardt stated that there had been confusion following the prior hearing regarding the dismissal of earlier filings and the procedural posture of the case. She reported that she intended to move the matter forward by filing a petition to probate a holographic will and to seek appointment of Lisa Cleveland as administrator, rather than continuing with the current administrator.

Ms. Erhardt further reported that her office had retained an expert to address issues related to the authenticity of the holographic will and that new petitions would be filed. The Court discussed the status of the current administrator and the existing bond and stated that the current administrator would remain in place pending further order of the Court.

The Court continued the matter to March 16, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and set for the same date a hearing on Status on Petition to Probate Holographic Will.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing
Off Calendar Dec-08-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The matter is ordered off calendar.

Judge:Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Cece. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-06-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Elizabeth Erhardt appeared via zoom for Lisa Clevland (petitioner).
Ronald Berman appeared via zoom for James Carter.

Discussion was held regarding the holographic will, that Mr. Berman's client will be filing a substitution of attorney, and that Ms. Erhardt will be filing her own petition on this matter.

The petition is denied without prejudice.

A status for hearing is set for December 8, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Josh Fredricks. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Espin. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-18-2025 Continued To Oct-06-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Elizabeth Erhardt for beneficiary Lisa Cleveland.

Ms. Erhardt reported to the Court that she had substituted in as counsel for Ms. Cleveland and requested that Mr. Berman withdraw as counsel for James W. Carter, Jr., the sole heir under intestate succession. She argued that Mr. Berman's prior representation of both Cleveland and Carter created a non-waivable conflict of interest, particularly because Mr. Carter contested the validity of the holographic will as a forgery. She stated that despite communications and citations to authority, Mr. Berman refused to withdraw absent a request from Mr. Carter. Ms. Erhardt indicated her intent to bring a motion to disqualify Mr. Berman and to seek sanctions against him and his law firm.

Ms. Erhardt requested additional time to prepare and file her motion. The Court continued the petition for letters of administration to October 6, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The Court instructed that when the motion is ready, Ms. Erhardt should contact the department to reserve a hearing date and serve proper notice.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
